source.title: Enhancement of the catalytic activity of Isopentenyl diphosphate isomerase (IDI) from Saccharomyces cerevisiae through random and site-directed mutagenesis

initial_request.research_problem: IDI is a rate-limiting enzyme in the lycopene biosynthetic pathway with low activity, short half-life, and weak substrate affinity. The goal is to enhance IDI catalytic activity through protein engineering to improve lycopene production in E. coli.
initial_request.target_object: Isopentenyl diphosphate isomerase (IDI) from Saccharomyces cerevisiae
initial_request.known_context: IDIs are limited by low enzyme activity, short half-life and weak substrate affinity

evolution_trajectory.state_before: IDI is a rate-limiting enzyme with low activity, short half-life, and weak substrate affinity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: IDI's low catalytic activity limits lycopene production; it is unclear which mutations could enhance activity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Directed evolution via error-prone PCR and lycopene-dependent color screening can identify IDI variants with improved activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design a directed evolution strategy using error-prone PCR and a lycopene-dependent color screening assay.
evolution_trajectory.observation: A strategy was designed to optimize IDI activity through error-prone PCR and color screening.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Implement the designed strategy by performing error-prone PCR.

evidence.quote_or_span: IDIs are limited by low enzyme activity, short half-life and weak substrate affinity
Directed evolution strategy (error-prone PCR) was utilized to optimize the activity of Saccharomyces cerevisiae IDI.
screening the development of a lycopene-dependent color reaction

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Directed evolution strategy designed but not yet executed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to generate a diverse library of IDI mutants to screen for improved variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Three cycles of error-prone PCR with library sizes of 10,000–15,000 will yield variants with enhanced activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform three cycles of error-prone PCR, each with a library size of 10,000–15,000 strains.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Three cycles of error-prone PCR were performed with library sizes of 10,000–15,000 strains.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Mutations identified from error-prone PCR lead to site-directed saturation mutagenesis on key residues.

evidence.quote_or_span: We performed three cycles of error-prone PCR, and the capacity of the mutation library was always 10,000–15,000 strains.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Saturation mutagenesis libraries constructed but best variants not yet selected.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which single mutations yield the highest lycopene production improvement?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: L141H, Y195F, and W256C are the best single mutations for enhancing lycopene production.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Identify L141H, Y195F, and W256C as the best single mutations based on lycopene production fold improvements.
evolution_trajectory.observation: L141H showed 1.05-fold improvement, Y195F showed 0.71-fold, and W256C showed 0.48-fold increase in lycopene production.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Combine best single mutations to create double and triple mutants.

evidence.quote_or_span: The L141H variant displayed 1.05-fold improvement in lycopene production compared to the wild type
Phe148 (which displayed a 0.71-fold increase)
W256C showed the highest increase in lycopene production (0.48-fold)
the triple mutant L141H/Y195F/W256C showed the highest improvement (3.13-fold of wild type)

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Best single mutations identified but combinatorial effects unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Will combining L141H, Y195F, and W256C yield additive or synergistic improvements?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The triple mutant L141H/Y195F/W256C will show the highest lycopene production improvement.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Create double and triple mutants using QuikChange II site-directed mutagenesis kit and measure lycopene production.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The triple mutant L141H/Y195F/W256C showed 3.13-fold improvement in lycopene production over wild type.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Characterize kinetic parameters of the triple mutant.

evidence.quote_or_span: the triple mutant L141H/Y195F/W256C showed the highest improvement (3.13-fold of wild type)

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Triple mutant shows improved lycopene production but kinetic basis unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How do the mutations affect kinetic parameters Km and kcat?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The triple mutant will exhibit decreased Km and increased kcat, leading to improved catalytic efficiency.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure kinetic parameters of wild-type and triple mutant IDI.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Km decreased by 10% (from 41.5 to 37.6 µM), kcat increased by 28% (from 8.27 to 10.57 s⁻¹), resulting in 1.40-fold improvement in kcat/Km.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate improved properties under varying pH conditions.

evidence.quote_or_span: The $K_{m}$ of mutant IDI decreased by 10% from $41.5 \pm 0.39$ to $37.6 \pm 0.17$ $\mu$ M
The $K_{cat}$ value increased from $8.27 \pm 0.06$ to $10.57 \pm 0.13$ s $^{-1}$
Triple-mutant IDI (L141H/Y195F/W256C) resulted in 1.40-fold improvement in the $K_{cat}/K_{m}$ value

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Kinetic improvements observed but pH-dependent activity and stability not assessed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does the triple mutant retain higher activity and stability across a range of pH values?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The triple mutant will show higher activity and better pH stability than wild-type across pH 5-10.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure activity and stability of wild-type and triple mutant IDI at various pH levels.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Triple mutant had higher activity than WT between pH 5.0 and 10.0, with optimum pH 7.5 for both, and better pH stability.

evidence.quote_or_span: IDI (L141H/Y195F/W256C) had higher activity than WT between pH 5.0 and pH 10.0.
IDI (L141H/Y195F/W256C) had better pH stability propertied compared to wild-type IDI
IDI (L141H/Y195F/W256C) had higher activity than WT between pH 5.0 and pH 10.0.
the optimum pH for both IDIs was 7.5

evolution_trajectory.state_before: pH validation complete; temperature effects unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does temperature affect the activity and stability of the triple mutant compared to wild-type?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The triple mutant will exhibit higher activity at optimal temperature and improved thermostability.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Determine optimal temperature and measure thermal stability (half-lives) of both IDI variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Optimal temperature for both was 35 °C; triple mutant activity >100% of WT; thermal stability of WT decreased more significantly; half-lives improved 2.6-16 fold.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test the triple mutant in fed-batch fermentation for lycopene production.

evidence.quote_or_span: the optimal temperature for both IDI variants was 35 °C
the activity of IDI (L141H/Y195F/W256C) was more than 100% compared to wild-type IDI at the optimal temperature
the thermal stability of wild-type IDI decreased more significantly than IDI (L141H/Y195F/W256C)
When determining thermal stability, 0.25 U enzyme solution underwent heat preservation at 25–55 °C for 1 h to determine the IDI activity under standard conditions.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Triple mutant shows improved in vitro properties; in vivo performance in fermentation unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can the improved enzymatic properties translate to higher lycopene production in a fed-batch fermentation?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Strain expressing triple mutant (CHL-2) will produce more lycopene than wild-type strain (CHL-1).
evolution_trajectory.decision: Conduct fed-batch fermentation comparing CHL-1 (wild-type IDI) and CHL-2 (triple mutant IDI).
evolution_trajectory.observation: CHL-2 had 1.8-fold higher lycopene production and 2.1-fold higher yield than CHL-1.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze lycopene accumulation profiles over time.

evidence.quote_or_span: the strain CHL-2 had 1.8-fold-improved lycopene production above the original strain CHL-1
the lycopene yield of CHL-2 was 2.1-fold higher than that of CHL-1
When the $OD_{600}$ reached 20, 0.05 mM IPTG was added to induce recombinant protein expression.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Fermentation yields measured; temporal accumulation pattern not yet analyzed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does the mutant sustain lycopene accumulation longer than wild-type?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: CHL-2 will exhibit continuous lycopene accumulation throughout fermentation, unlike CHL-1 which plateaus.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare lycopene accumulation profiles of CHL-1 and CHL-2 over 90 h.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Lycopene accumulation in CHL-1 lasted 70 h, while CHL-2 accumulated continuously throughout 90 h.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Perform structural modeling to explain mechanistic basis of improvements.

evidence.quote_or_span: lycopene accumulation in CHL-1 lasted for 70 h, while lycopene accumulation in CHL-2 was continuous throughout the whole fermentation process

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Experimental improvements observed but structural rationale missing.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the molecular mechanisms by which L141H, Y195F, and W256C enhance activity and stability?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutations enhance substrate binding and stability through hydrogen bonding and hydrophobic interactions.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Build homology model of IDI using E. coli IDI (2ICJ) as template and perform molecular docking with IPP.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Binding energy increased from 6.87 to 8.87 kcal/mol; L141H forms hydrogen bond with IPP β-phosphate; Y195F and W256C enhance hydrophobic interactions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude that mutations are distinct and additive, contributing to enhanced activity and stability.

evidence.quote_or_span: in silico structural modelling and molecular docking were performed by using the 3D structure of IPP isomerase from E. coli (PDB ID: 2ICJ)
The molecular docking results revealed that the binding energy between the substrate and the mutant enzyme was increased to 8.87 kcal/mol from the original 6.87 kcal/mol
L141H substitution showed His-141 participated in the construction of the active pocket, which enlarged the IDI catalytic domain
Due to hydrophobicity of Phe-195 and Cys-256, Ala-106, Phe-107 and Cys-139 could fixed substrate better

This suggested that enhanced enzymatic activity was primarily a result of two main factors: (1) improved affinity between the substrate and enzyme caused by mutations (L141H, Y195F and W256C) and (2) increased enzyme stability due to mutations within the hydrogen bond and hydrophobic interaction

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Structural insights gained; overall contribution of mutations summarized.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Are the mutations additive and distinct in their contributions?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: L141H, Y195F, and W256C are distinct mutations that additively enhance activity through improved substrate affinity and enzyme stability.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Conclude that enhanced activity results from improved substrate affinity and increased enzyme stability due to the mutations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Mutations are distinct and contribute considerably; enhanced activity from improved affinity and stability.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Acknowledge limitations of error-prone PCR for further improvement.

evidence.quote_or_span: This suggested that L141H, Y195F and W256C were distinct mutations that contribute considerably to the enhanced activity of mutant IDI.

This suggested that enhanced enzymatic activity was primarily a result of two main factors: (1) improved affinity between the substrate and enzyme caused by mutations (L141H, Y195F and W256C) and (2) increased enzyme stability due to mutations within the hydrogen bond and hydrophobic interaction

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Successful engineering achieved but further improvement may be limited.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can error-prone PCR alone substantially increase IDI activity further?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Error-prone PCR has inherent deficiencies that limit substantial further increase in IDI activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge that other techniques are needed for further optimization.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Due to the inherent deficiency of error-prone PCR, it was difficult to substantially increase IDI activity.

evidence.quote_or_span: due to the inherent deficiency of error-prone PCR, it was difficult to substantially increase IDI activity

success_verification.final_conclusion: Triple mutant L141H/Y195F/W256C significantly enhances IDI catalytic activity, leading to 3.13-fold lycopene production improvement in vivo, with improved kinetic parameters, pH and temperature stability, and sustained fermentation performance. Mutations act additively through enhanced substrate affinity and enzyme stability.
limitations: Error-prone PCR inherent deficiency limits substantial further increase in IDI activity; other techniques needed for further optimization.
